summary: Phase 6 (Calendar) backend + frontend e2e testing complete. 20/20 backend pytest cases pass (auth all 5 roles, regression smoke for property/admin/broker/employee/subscription/cms/notifications routes after Depends() patch, calendar block/unblock/unified-view/iCal export/external calendars CRUD + sync, booking blocked-by-manual-block guard). Host calendar UI loads, property selector populated, calendar grid renders, prev/next month navigation works, block-dates form submits successfully, external iCal add/sync/remove all hit correct API endpoints with 200 responses.

minor.issue: Returns 307 redirect; only /api/properties/search returns 200. Make sure frontend doesn't break on 307 (it follows automatically) but listing root with no slash returns 405. Consider unifying.

integration_issues.issue: After successfully blocking dates in a future month (e.g., Nov 2026) while the calendar grid is showing the current month (May 2026), the 'Block Dates' side panel still shows 'No manual blocks yet' because the unified-view query is scoped to the visible month. The block IS created (verified via API) but invisible to the user unless they navigate to the month containing the block. Recommendation: either fetch all upcoming manual blocks for the side list, or auto-navigate the calendar to the new block's month after creation.

action_items: Optional: improve Block Dates side-panel to surface manual blocks across all months, not just the visible month, so hosts get immediate feedback after creating a future block.
Optional: normalise GET /api/properties (without trailing slash) — currently 405; either add an alias or document /properties/search as the canonical listing endpoint.
critical_code_review_comments: calendar_routes.sync_external_calendar uses synchronous `requests.get(...)` inside an async handler which blocks the event loop. Switch to httpx.AsyncClient for non-blocking iCal fetches; current implementation is fine for the demo but will not scale.

calendar_routes.add_external_calendar awaits sync_external_calendar inline before responding — slow/unreachable iCal URLs will delay the API response (timeout=15s). Consider firing sync as a background task (FastAPI BackgroundTasks) and returning immediately with sync_status='pending'.
BlockDateRequest validation relies solely on date.today() comparison — confirm container TZ matches expected business TZ (Asia/Kolkata) to avoid edge-of-day false positives.
blocked_dates and external_calendars collections lack indexes on (property_id, start_date, end_date) and (property_id, owner_id) — add Mongo indexes for performance once data volume grows.
test_credentials: All 5 seeded roles in /app/memory/test_credentials.md verified working. Host: host@propnest.com / host123 used for calendar tests.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded property prop_1778064931.547249 (Test Beach Villa, Goa). All TEST_-prefixed manual blocks and external calendars created during testing have been cleaned up via API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_phase6_calendar.py covers Phase 1-6 smoke + Phase 6 calendar deeply. Run via `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_phase6_calendar.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Container date is May 6, 2026 — keep using 2026-08+ dates for blocking. Razorpay/MSG91/SendGrid still mocked. Booking POST requires field name `number_of_guests` (not num_guests). /api/properties/ root returns 307; use /api/properties/search for listing. Frontend at /host/calendar requires host login; data-testids all present and reliable.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues found. The 'block created but not visible in side list' UX gap is by design (month-scoped unified-view) but confusing — recommend updating UI to fetch a broader window for the manual-blocks summary panel.
